chunk_summary: This section focuses on dysglycemia screening in pediatric obesity, outlining diagnostic thresholds, testing protocols, and management strategies for prediabetes and diabetes, with actionable pathways for follow-up care.
learning_objectives: Understand the diagnostic thresholds for diabetes and prediabetes in pediatric obesity.
Learn the appropriate use of OGTT and its operational details in dysglycemia evaluation.
Identify management strategies for prediabetes and diabetes, including lifestyle interventions and referral protocols.
Recognize the importance of repeat testing and risk stratification in pediatric dysglycemia.
key_facts: Diabetes diagnostic thresholds: fasting plasma glucose ≥126 mg/dL, 2-hour OGTT glucose ≥200 mg/dL, or HbA1c ≥6.5%.
Prediabetes thresholds: fasting glucose 100–125 mg/dL (ADA) or 110–125 mg/dL (WHO); 2-hour OGTT glucose 140–199 mg/dL.
OGTT protocol: 1.75 g/kg glucose (max 75 g), dissolved in water, consumed within 5 minutes, with a 2-hour sample collected.
Annual retesting is acceptable for stable low-risk prediabetes, but 3–6 month intervals are recommended for high-risk cases.
Pancreatic autoantibody testing is advised when considering youth-onset type 2 diabetes, per RSSDI recommendations.
reference_quote: Diabetes can be diagnosed by fasting plasma glucose ≥126 mg/dL, 2-hour glucose ≥200 mg/dL during OGTT, or HbA1c ≥6.5%.
document_key_topics: Diagnostic thresholds for diabetes and prediabetes
Management and follow-up for prediabetes and diabetes
